classification_notes: Classification uses regex keyword matching on video titles and channel names, with priority-ordered category matching and channel-specific heuristics. Categories are checked in order: coding_agents > video_gen > voice_audio > creative_ai > agent_arch > local_ai > philosophy_ethics > ml_fundamentals > llm_models (catch-all). Of 1481 total videos, 22 false positives were removed (1.5%), leaving 1459 classified videos. 184 videos (12.6%) fell into 'other_ai' with no keyword match. These are typically general commentary, reaction videos, or tangentially AI-related content that doesn't fit a specific technical category. False positive identification uses channel-name heuristics: NBC News/MS NOW political content with 'agents' meaning law enforcement, Late Show with Stephen Colbert variety segments, OnlyPlants (plant care), Don't Tell Comedy (stand-up). Channel-specific rules handle edge cases: Jake Van Clief maps to coding_agents, Two Minute Papers to ml_fundamentals, Claudius Papirus to ml_fundamentals, Dylan Patel to philosophy_ethics, Dario Amodei to philosophy_ethics, Matthew Berman to llm_models, AI Explained to llm_models.
